stories.headline: US, Iran Fail to Reach Peace Agreement After Marathon Talks
stories.summary: The US and Iran ended direct talks in Pakistan without an agreement, putting a fragile ceasefire at risk and raising doubts over efforts to end a six-week war that has killed thousands and disrupted global energy supplies.

stories.headline: US and Iran Have Failed to Reach an Agreement, JD Vance Says
stories.summary:
The US and Iran failed to reach an agreement after marathon talks in Pakistan on the weekend, a major setback to finding a lasting resolution to the six-week war in the Middle East.

Vice President JD Vance said negotiators will return to the US without a deal after Iran failed to give a commitment it would not seek a nuclear weapon. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran war live: Ghalibaf blames US for failure of Pakistan ceasefire talks
stories.summary: Iran's Parliamentary ⁠Speaker Mohammad ⁠Baqer Ghalibaf says the US failed to gain the trust ​of Tehran's delegation.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: US and Iran fail to reach deal after marathon talks
stories.summary: Negotiations foundered over Tehran’s unwillingness to concede ground on nuclear weapons, says JD Vance

stories.headline: Penny Wong calls failed peace talks between US and Iran ‘disappointing’ and urges resumption

stories.headline: Pakistan urges US and Iran to uphold ceasefire after talks end
stories.summary: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has insisted the US and Iran must uphold their ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: 'We need real peace': Easter truce fails to lift grim mood in war-torn Ukraine
stories.summary: Easter festivities are muted in Kharkiv as Ukrainians expect fighting to flare up again after a weekend truce.

stories.headline: US and Iran end peace and ceasefire talks in Pakistan without agreement
stories.summary: The United States and Iran did not reach an agreement at the end of the historic talks in Pakistan, but the fact that the two sides engaged in face-to-face, direct talks after hours in which the Pakistani side was shuttling between them marked a certain breakthrough in negotiations.

stories.headline: Palestinians appalled as Israel approves settlements in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: Israel has approved 34 new settlements in the occupied West Bank, Palestinians are terrified they will lose their land.

stories.headline: Hungarians vote in hard-fought election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years
stories.summary: Rightwing leader trails in polls to Péter Magyar, despite support from JD Vance on recent visitHungarians are heading to the ballot boxes to vote in a hard-fought parliamentary election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power and potentially reshape the central European country’s relations with the EU, Moscow and Washington.In the campaign, Orbán – the EU’s longest-serving leader – has trailed in the polls as he faces an unprecedente

stories.headline: Oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
stories.summary: Three supertankers laden with oil exit waterway amid global energy crunch, shipping data shows.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: Accusations that Mr. Swalwell, a congressman running for governor in California, sexually assaulted a former staffer and behaved inappropriately with other women have upended the race.

stories.headline: Benin holds presidential election four months after failed coup
stories.summary: As president Patrice Talon steps down after a decade, the west African country’s finance minister is favourite to winThis Sunday, just four months after a failed coup, Benin heads to the polls for a presidential election that feels more like a coronation than a contest.Patrice Talon, the businessman turned politician who has been president since 2016, is ineligible to run again after serving two five-year terms. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
stories.summary: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, and early figures point to a record turnout of voters.

stories.headline: Police clear Irish fuel protesters from several locations
stories.summary: A cabinet meeting will be held on Sunday where an update on ongoing fuel crisis talks will be given.

stories.headline: China Unveils Taiwan Goodwill Steps After Opposition Talks
stories.summary: China announced a series of policy measures to demonstrate “goodwill” toward Taiwan on Sunday, following a landmark meeting between President Xi Jinping and Taiwan opposition leader Cheng Li-wun.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia Says East-West Pipeline Restored to Full Capacity
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia has restored the full pumping capacity of its East-West pipeline to 7 million barrels a day, rehabilitating a vital link for oil exports via the Red Sea.

stories.headline: Benin holds presidential election amid deteriorating security situation
stories.summary: Benin is facing harsh economic conditions and security challenges that its new leader will have to address.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia says key oil pipeline back to full capacity after attacks
stories.summary: The Ministry of Energy says the East-West pipeline is back to pumping about 7 million barrels per day.

stories.headline: Hungarians vote as PM Orban faces toughest election challenge in years
stories.summary: The parliamentary election could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year hold on power.

stories.headline: Malnourished Afghan minors trapped as two wars cut off supplies
At least 30 feared dead in crush at Haitian tourist site
stories.summary: The incident occurred at a "tourist event attended by many young people", Haiti's prime minister says.

stories.headline: Why this disillusioned Trump voter spends hours searching Epstein files
stories.summary: Many who support the president have expressed frustration with how Trump's former attorney general Pam Bondi handled the Epstein files.

stories.headline: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.headline: Event Bets Pose a Problem for Wall Street Firms Looking to Trade
stories.summary: Prediction markets are booming. Wagers on everything from the return of Jesus Christ to who will win the World Cup have driven weekly volumes on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi into the billions of dollars.

stories.headline: India Forex Regulator Criticizes Banks’ Rupee Arbitrage Trades
stories.summary: A senior Reserve Bank of India official criticized foreign-exchange market makers for their role in aggravating the rupee’s weakness during the Middle East tensions, as the regulator keeps up its tough messaging stance in its defense of the currency.

stories.headline: Why Water Bonds Could Help More Funding Flow Into Africa
stories.summary: Blue and green bonds have emerged as a potential way for African nations to close a massive financing gap. Bloomberg's Yinka Ibukun explains how water bonds could help play a role. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Japan to Work With Asian Nations to Ease Oil Bottleneck: Akazawa
stories.summary: Japan will work closely with Asian countries where essential petroleum-based products including medical equipment are produced, economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said in a Sunday debate program on broadcaster NHK.

stories.headline: What the ‘Real Housewives’ Think About Congress’s Reality TV Drama
stories.summary: A visit to the Capitol by stars of the “Real Housewives” offered a glimpse into how the nation’s elected representatives mimic the gossipy world of reality TV. Nobody appears to relish the similarities.

stories.headline: Push to Expel Swalwell Could Touch Off Chain Reaction of House Removal Votes
stories.summary: The House could move within days to consider expelling Representative Eric Swalwell, Democrat of California, in light of sexual assault allegations against him. There could be more to come for both parties.

stories.headline: More than 500 people arrested at London rally for Palestine Action
stories.summary: Protest organisers accuse UK government of complicity in Gaza genocide as arrests made at Trafalgar Square rally.

stories.headline: Netanyahu next to Middle East map: ‘We strangled them and have more to do’
stories.summary: Standing by a map, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu describes how six countries wanted to strangle us’.

Gas price surge kick-starts US demand for used EVs
Japan's independent power retailers halt corporate contracts as costs spike
Trump's latest global tariffs challenged in federal court Friday
Sumitomo Forestry bets big on US housing market, eyeing local majors
EU’s largest economies vs top US states: How do they compare in GDP?
stories.summary: Top EU countries and US states appear one after the other in economic size rankings. However, US states surpass the top EU economies in GDP per capita, both in nominal terms and purchasing power.

stories.headline: US and Iranian officials begin high-stakes peace talks in Pakistan to resolve war
stories.summary: Three party talks with the US, Iran and Pakistan have begun after a reduction in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and other preconditions being met, according to Iran's IRNA news agency.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia exchange prisoners ahead of Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: The warring sides exchanged 175 prisoners of war each in a mediation facilitated by the United Arab Emirates, officials from both nations said.

stories.headline: U.S. Intelligence Shows China Taking a More Active Role in Iran War
stories.summary: China may have shipped missiles to Iran, and Beijing is allowing some companies to sell Tehran supplies that can be used in military production, American officials said.

stories.headline: EU airline industry warns of fuel shortages if Strait of Hormuz stays closed
stories.summary: Airports Council International (ACI) Europe said its members had "increasing concerns" about the availability of jet fuel.

stories.headline: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
stories.summary: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

stories.headline: A Panicked Race for Barrels Grips the Global Oil Market
stories.summary: While investors focused on the fragile Iranian ceasefire last week, a desperate scramble for cargoes has been playing out in the oil market, as traders and refiners scour the globe for immediately available supplies.

stories.headline: Islamabad Peace Talks Latest
stories.summary: Pakistan is playing host to US and Iranian delegates in Islamabad. Pakistani officials are optimistic, but uncertaity remains around how much both parties will be willing to concede. Bloomberg's Pakistan Bureau Chief Faseeh Mangi reports from central Islamabad for Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Eric Swalwell, California governor candidate, accused of sexual assault by ex-staffer
stories.summary: Supporters of Rep. Eric Swalwell’s campaign withdrew support, including two congressmen who chaired his campaign and two senators who endorsed his bid. Swalwell denied the allegations.

stories.headline: Irish cabinet meeting to be held to discuss ongoing fuel protests
stories.summary: Hundreds of petrol stations in the Republic of Ireland have run out of fuel as the protests and blockades continued for a fifth day.

stories.headline: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
stories.summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

stories.headline: RBNZ to Announce Next Steps on Cash-Rate Decision Transparency
stories.summary: New Zealand’s central bank will soon announce steps to increase the transparency of its monetary policy decisions, Governor Anna Breman said.

stories.headline: Appeals Court Allows Work on Trump’s Ballroom Project for Now, but Seeks Clarity on Scope
stories.summary: A three-judge panel allowed construction on the ballroom to proceed until April 17, but asked a lower court to untangle the details of the president’s plans.

stories.headline: Postal Service, Under Pressure, Now Faces Trump’s Mail Ballot Order
stories.summary: President Trump’s executive order, which has been challenged as unconstitutional, would limit the Postal Service to sending only the ballots of voters deemed eligible.

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: The high-stakes diplomacy that led to Pakistan hosting US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Critical minerals, cryptocurrency, and CIA co-operation - how Asim Munir, Trump’s “favourite field marshal”, may have pulled off the diplomatic coup of the century.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday.

stories.headline: Eliot Engel, a prominent voice on foreign affairs in the House, dies at 79
stories.summary: The New York Democrat helped lead the first impeachment inquiry into President Donald Trump. He served for 16 terms before losing his reelection bid in 2020.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds
stories.summary: Powerful storm brings destruction, while temperatures soar in Vietnam and torrential rain lashes South KoreaSevere Tropical Cyclone Maila, currently in the Solomon Sea, is expected to continue moving south-westwards over the coming days. stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.summary: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: Hot in the city: Energy crisis tests Singapore's air-con addiction
stories.summary: The rise in energy prices has hit Asia particularly hard as many nations are heavily reliant on Gulf oil.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.headline: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.summary: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.headline: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
stories.summary: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

stories.headline: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
stories.summary: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

stories.headline: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.headline: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound

stories.headline: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
stories.summary: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.
